source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
An inclined plane ABC is placed on a horizontal surface. The height BC of the inclined plane is 4h, and the length of the base AB is 3h. Point D is directly above point A and at the same height as point C. Air resistance is neglected, and the acceleration due to gravity is g.

1. At point C, a small ball is projected horizontally to the left with an initial velocity $v_0$ and then falls back onto the inclined plane. Determine the magnitude of the velocity of the ball when it is at the farthest distance from the inclined plane.
2. At point C, a small ball is projected horizontally to the left with an initial velocity $v_0$. Determine the magnitude of the velocity of the ball when it lands on the inclined plane and the tangent of the angle between the velocity and the horizontal direction.
3. At point D, a small ball is projected horizontally to the right and strikes the inclined plane perpendicularly. Determine the magnitude of the horizontal and vertical displacements of the ball at this instant.

issues: Top-level image_paths and metadata.image_paths are inconsistent.

image_dependency: requires_image=true and text_dominant=false indicate the sample is intended to rely on a diagram.
The question uses named points A, B, C, D on an inclined plane and asks about perpendicular impact and farthest distance from the plane, which are strongly spatial/diagram-linked relations.
Image asset exists and registry_passed=true, so visual support is available.
Dependency is not maximal because the text also specifies key geometric relations and lengths (BC=4h, AB=3h, D above A and level with C), so the problem may still be solvable from text alone.
multi_step: The problem has three numbered sub-questions, each requiring separate reasoning.
Each part combines projectile-motion kinematics with incline geometry/spatial constraints rather than a single direct lookup.
Part 2 and Part 3 require solving for landing/impact conditions and then deriving velocity components or displacements, which is clearly multi-step.
Question text is long (question_char_length=1011), consistent with nontrivial multi-condition reasoning.
verifiability: answer_present=true and the provided answers are explicit closed-form expressions and values, which are checkable.
The outputs are numerical/algebraic targets (speed, tangent value, horizontal displacement, vertical displacement), suitable for rule-based or symbolic verification.
Verifiability is slightly reduced because answer_text bundles results for three sub-questions in compact form without explicit labels beyond line order.

risk_flags: Top-level image_paths and metadata.image_paths are inconsistent.
answer_text packs multiple sub-answer items compactly, which may create alignment ambiguity during evaluation.

decision_reasons: Core assets are present and usable: question text, answer text, and primary image all exist, and registry_passed=true.
Initial scoring is strong overall, with high multi-step and verifiability scores and substantial image relevance.

The recorded issues are follow-up quality risks rather than core semantic loss: image-path inconsistency appears to be metadata-level, and the compact multi-part answer may need alignment handling but is still recoverable.
Given the collection-stage high-recall policy, this sample is strong enough to retain in the candidate pool rather than downrank.

normalization_record.normalized_question_text:
An inclined plane ABC is placed on a horizontal surface. The height BC of the inclined plane is 4h, and the length of the base AB is 3h. Point D is directly above point A and at the same height as point C. Air resistance is neglected, and the acceleration due to gravity is g.

1. At point C, a small ball is projected horizontally to the left with an initial velocity $v_0$ and then falls back onto the inclined plane. Determine the magnitude of the velocity of the ball when it is at the farthest distance from the inclined plane.
2. At point C, a small ball is projected horizontally to the left with an initial velocity $v_0$. Determine the magnitude of the velocity of the ball when it lands on the inclined plane and the tangent of the angle between the velocity and the horizontal direction.
3. At point D, a small ball is projected horizontally to the right and strikes the inclined plane perpendicularly. Determine the magnitude of the horizontal and vertical displacements of the ball at this instant.
normalization_notes: Geometry problem uses labeled diagram points; text preserved without adding inferred details.
